article_meta.title: ‘President Trump,” reports NPR, “says he hasn’t decided whether he’ll launch a military strike on Iran.” This is a remar

reasoning: The summary largely neutralizes the opinion piece’s loaded rhetoric and attributes evaluations to the author. It avoids adopting the article’s emotive framing, with only minor phrasing that could imply agreement (e.g., "without proper legislative oversight").

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral, attributive verbs (critiques, argues, contends) and avoids the article’s emotive language.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims are framed as the author’s arguments or observations from cited reporting.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Mostly attributed; a slight implication in "without proper legislative oversight" could read as the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming wording beyond the source’s claims, and rhetoric is toned down.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Does not infer motives or mental states.
custom_reasoning.slant: Reflects the single-opinion nature of the source without exaggerating one side over omitted ones.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures core arguments and context; leaves out some rhetorical flourishes but not substantive perspectives.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal and mostly within attributed context (e.g., "fundamental shift").
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral terminology overall; minor loaded terms appear in attributed context.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone matches the source’s critical stance but is restrained and attributed.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No undue deference to elites or dismissal of other perspectives.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Noticeably more neutral than the source, with clear attribution of judgments.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Represents the author’s viewpoint and notes NPR as the reported source; no other perspectives present in the article to include.
